content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Donna Darnell 
700 Randall 
Ridgecrest, CA 93555 
Dear Ms. Darnell: 
January 19, 1990 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-697 
You have requested confirmation of my telephone advice 
provided to you on behalf of Supervisor Roy Ashburn concerning the 
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.~/ 
Your letter, received December 19, 1989, correctly summarizes 
my advice that if the property which was donated for a fundraising 
auction is appraised at a value in excess of $1,000, you should 
contact the Commission for further advice. 
A copy of your letter 
is enclosed for your convenience. 
In the event that the property in question is appraised in 
excess of $1,000, Regulation 18531 (copy enclosed) requires that 
Supervisor Ashburn's committee pay to the donor the amount in 
excess of $1,000 or else return the property to the donor. 
Since 
the property has been sold, the appropriate action would be to pay 
the excess amount to the donor. 
This payment ordinarily must be 
made within 14 days of receipt of the in-kind contribution.~/ 
Based on the facts presented in your letter, it appears likely 
that the committee will not have the results of the appraisal 
until after expiration of this 14-day deadline. 
In that case, if 
an amount in excess of the $1,000 contribution limit is to be 
returned to the donor, we advise that the payment be made as soon 
as possible after the appraisal. 
1/ Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et ~ 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
~/ Regulation 18531 was amended December 13, 1989, to provide for 
return of excessive contributions within 14 days of receipt. 
These amendments have been adopted by the Commission and are 
pending review by the Office of Administrative Law. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Donna Darnell 
Our File No. A-89-697 
Page 2 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: 
Alice Hughes 
Political Reform Consultant 






California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Donna Darnell 
700 Randall 
Ridgecrest, CA 
93555 
Dear Ms. Darnell: 
December 22, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-697 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the political Reform Act on December 19, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322,5660
